expectations.text: Pack directory exists at docs/references/tanstack-router-v1/ (or .claude/knowledge/ fallback)
expectations.evidence: Pack copied to outputs/pack/ with all expected topic files.

expectations.text: INDEX.md exists with required frontmatter fields (title, concept, mode=framework, version, updated)
expectations.evidence: Frontmatter has title='TanStack Router v1 Reference Pack', concept='tanstack-router-v1', mode=framework, version=v1, updated=2026-04-17.

expectations.text: INDEX.md has a 'When to consult this pack' section that names concrete tasks (routing, loaders, search params)
expectations.evidence: Section lists specific signals: createFileRoute, Outlet, Link, useNavigate, loader, beforeLoad, validateSearch, src/routes/, main.tsx/vite.config.ts.

expectations.text: INDEX.md has a 'Version-specific warnings' section listing at least 2 specific old-pattern -> new-pattern replacements
expectations.evidence: Lists 10 specific replacements: react-router-dom→@tanstack/react-router, new RootRoute→createRootRoute, useSearchParams→validateSearch+Route.useSearch, etc.

expectations.text: INDEX.md's Topics list has 3-10 topics and every bullet resolves to a real topic file
expectations.evidence: 5 topics, all resolving: route-definitions.md, loaders.md, search-params.md, migration-from-react-router.md, common-mistakes.md.

expectations.text: metadata.yaml exists and declares mode: framework
expectations.evidence: metadata.yaml has mode: framework, sources list with 24 URLs, topics list, claude_md_pointer.

expectations.text: raw/ directory exists with downloaded source files from TanStack Router docs
expectations.evidence: raw/ directory contains 27 downloaded markdown files.

expectations.text: At least one topic file includes a 'Common mistakes' block contrasting a training-data default against the correct pattern
expectations.evidence: common-mistakes.md has 14 numbered WRONG/RIGHT pairs plus a 'Quick audit checklist'.

expectations.text: CLAUDE.md has or grew a '## Framework/API References' section with a TanStack Router v1 bullet
expectations.evidence: Section exists with TanStack Router v1 bullet pointing at INDEX.md.

expectations.text: The CLAUDE.md 'Consult when' phrase is specific — mentions routing/loaders/search-params
expectations.evidence: Phrase: 'writing or modifying routes in src/routes/, using createFileRoute/createRootRoute/createRouter, handling route loaders (loader, beforeLoad, loaderDeps, staleTime), or reading/writing URL search params'.

expectations.text: Pack directory exists at docs/references/deepgram-tts-aura-2/ (or similar deepgram-tts slug)
expectations.evidence: Pack at deepgram-tts-aura-2/ (agent corrected the user's 'v3' misnomer).

expectations.text: INDEX.md exists with frontmatter declaring mode: api
expectations.evidence: Frontmatter: mode: api, version: Aura-2.

expectations.text: INDEX.md 'When to consult' section names concrete API tasks (request bodies, auth, streaming, error handling)
expectations.evidence: Names synthesizing audio, configuring voice agent speech output, opening streaming socket, picking voice/model id, handling auth.

expectations.text: A topic file covers request/response format with exact field names verbatim from docs
expectations.evidence: wire-format-rest.md has verbatim request shape {'text':'...'}, full query-param table with all valid encoding/container/sample_rate combinations, pulled from official docs.

expectations.text: At least one topic file has a 'Common mistakes' block calling out confusable field names (e.g. voice_id vs model)
expectations.evidence: INDEX 'Version-specific warnings' and wire-format-rest both call out voice_id (not a field), model in body (wrong location), voice.* nested object (doesn't exist).

expectations.text: Every wire-format claim in topic files cites a deepgram.com (or developers.deepgram.com) URL at point of use
expectations.evidence: Multiple developers.deepgram.com/docs/... source URLs cited inline at each claim in wire-format-rest.md.

expectations.text: metadata.yaml exists and declares mode: api
expectations.evidence: metadata.yaml present with mode: api.

expectations.text: No raw/ directory — api mode is synthesis, not a mirror
expectations.evidence: raw/ absent (confirmed by test -d).

expectations.text: Quick-start topic has both a curl example AND an SDK example (Python or Node)
expectations.evidence: quick-start.md reported to have minimal runnable Python and curl examples for both REST and WebSocket.

expectations.text: CLAUDE.md has a Deepgram TTS v3 bullet with a specific consult-when phrase
expectations.evidence: Bullet: 'Consult when writing any code that calls Deepgram text-to-speech (REST POST /v1/speak or WebSocket wss://api.deepgram.com/v1/speak), picking a voice/model id, setting up the voice agent's TTS output, or handling Deepgram auth.'

expectations.text: Pack directory exists at docs/references/modern-idiomatic-typescript/ (or similar slug)
expectations.evidence: Pack at docs/references/modern-idiomatic-typescript/ — matches skill's slug convention.

expectations.text: INDEX.md exists with frontmatter declaring mode: idioms
expectations.evidence: Frontmatter: mode: idioms, version: 'TypeScript 5.x (through 5.7), Biome 2.x'.

expectations.text: INDEX.md 'Version-specific warnings' section lists at least 3 training-data defaults that the pack overrides
expectations.evidence: Lists 9 training-data defaults: enum, namespace, as cast, any, class-heavy, ESLint+Prettier, type Id=string, interface-for-props-by-default, strict-only tsconfig.

expectations.text: Topic files include 'Prefer X over Y' substitutions with side-by-side do/don't code examples
expectations.evidence: satisfies-over-assertions.md has a 'Do / Don't' block with three side-by-side examples (satisfies vs variable-annotation vs as cast).

expectations.text: Topic files cover version-specific features (satisfies, const type parameters, etc.)
expectations.evidence: satisfies-over-assertions.md covers TS 4.9 satisfies; pack also has branded-types.md, no-enum-discriminated-unions.md, unknown-over-any.md.

expectations.text: Topic files explicitly cover anti-patterns (enum, namespace, any casts)
expectations.evidence: Dedicated files: no-enum-discriminated-unions.md, no-namespace-no-classes.md, unknown-over-any.md.

expectations.text: Every prescription in topic files cites a source URL
expectations.evidence: Every load-bearing rule in satisfies-over-assertions.md has an inline URL (totaltypescript.com, typescriptlang.org handbook, 2ality.com, effectivetypescript.com).

expectations.text: Pack references multiple distinct authoritative voices across topics — not resting on a single blog source
expectations.evidence: Satisfies file alone cites Matt Pocock, Axel Rauschmayer (2ality), Dan Vanderkam (Effective TypeScript). Agent report: 'Every prescription...triangulated across 2-3 independent authoritative sources.'

expectations.text: No raw/ directory — idioms mode is pure synthesis
expectations.evidence: raw/ absent (confirmed by test -d).

expectations.text: CLAUDE.md has a 'modern idiomatic TypeScript' bullet with consult-when phrased around writing or refactoring TypeScript
expectations.evidence: Bullet: 'Consult when writing or refactoring any .ts/.tsx file, choosing lint/format tooling, or editing tsconfig.json — anchors to current practice (satisfies, discriminated unions, branded types, Biome) and overrides training-data defaults like enum, namespace, any, and ESLint+Prettier scaffolds.'
